revision.text:
[[ja:羽黒神社 (倉敷市)]] {{Citation needed|date=November 2010}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Haguro Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Haguro Shinto shrine sanctuary.JPG | location={{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama|1=Okayama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|qid=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki|1=Kurashiki}}, Tamashima Chuocho 1-12-1 | coordinates={{Coord|34.54336111111111|N|133.67166666666665|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Main deity= (Primary deity) {{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|qid=Q56344982|lt=Tamayorihime|1=Tamayori-hime}}
(Associated deities) {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}}, {{ill|Kotoshironushi|en|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} | Foundation=Year Manji 1 (1658) | Shrine Rank=Former Gō-sha (郷社) | Annual festival=Tenjin Festival, Autumn Grand Festival | Main hall= | ISO=JP-33 | Latitude degrees=34 | Latitude minutes=32 | Latitude seconds=36.1 | Longitude degrees=133 | Longitude minutes=40 | Longitude seconds=18 | Label position=left | Map=Japan Okayama#Japan | prefecture={{ill|Kurashiki|qid=Q467487|1=Kurashiki}} }} '''Haguro Shrine''' (羽黒神社, Haguro Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Tamashima|ja|玉島地域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamashima|1=Tamashima Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572516}} district of {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|qid=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki|1=Kurashiki}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|qid=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. == History == This shrine is situated atop Mount Haguro in the old town area of Tamashima, which was once a small island in the {{ill|Seto Inland Sea|en|Seto Inland Sea|qid=Q231312|lt=Seto Inland Sea|1=Seto Inland Sea}}. In the first year of the Manji era (1658), {{ill|Mizutani Katsutaka|ja|水谷勝隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizutani Katsutaka|1=Mizunoya Katsutaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11549155}}, the lord of {{ill|Bitchū-Matsuyama Domain|en|Bitchū-Matsuyama Domain|qid=Q3034422|lt=Bitchu Matsuyama Domain|1=Bitchū-Matsuyama Domain}}, established the shrine during the reclamation of the Tamashima region by enshrining a {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|qid=Q195793|lt=divided spirit|1=Bunrei}} from {{ill|Dewa Shrine|en|Dewa Shrine|qid=Q11395802|lt=Dewa Shrine|1=Dewa Shrine}} on {{ill|Three Mountains of Dewa|en|Three Mountains of Dewa|qid=Q1155726|lt=Mount Haguro (Yamagata)|1=Three Mountains of Dewa}} in {{ill|Dewa Province|en|Dewa Province|qid=Q876032|lt=Dewa Province|1=Dewa Province}}. The shrine later became a center of faith for the residents of Tamashima Port, which thrived as a commercial port, and for merchants traveling aboard {{ill|Kitamaebune|en|Kitamaebune|qid=Q3440099|lt=Kitamaebune|1=Kitamaebune}} trading ships. In 1665, the second lord of the domain, {{ill|Mizutani Katsumune|ja|水谷勝宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizutani Katsumune|1=Mizunoya Katsumune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11549150}}, rebuilt the shrine structures. The current main hall was reconstructed in the third year of the Kaei era (1845), while the haiden (worship hall) was rebuilt in the fourth year of the Ansei era (1852). From 1970 to 1973, the shrine underwent expansion as part of a project to handle wartime air-raid shelter remnants, resulting in the construction of the Haguro Shrine Assembly Hall, whose roof now serves as an expanded precinct. == Deities == {{Citation needed|Tamayorihime, Susanoo, Okuninushi, Kotoshironushi|date=November 2010}} == Divine Virtues == {{Citation needed|Blessings of prosperity, matchmaking, and commercial success|date=November 2010}} === Festivals === {{Citation needed| * 2nd Saturday and Sunday of May: Festival of {{ill|Sumiyoshi Shrine|en|Sumiyoshi Shrine|qid=Q32512|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine (Japan)|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} and Mizutani Shrine * 1st Friday and Saturday of August: Tenjin Festival * 2nd Saturday and Sunday of October: Autumn Grand Festival A Seven Lucky Gods festival is held annually on the first Sunday of April during the Prayer Festival, as the main hall is surrounded by shrines to the Seven Lucky Gods.|date=November 2010}} == Access == * About 2 km south of {{ill|Shin-Kurashiki Station|en|Shin-Kurashiki Station|qid=Q1067908|lt=Shin-Kurashiki Station|1=Shin-Kurashiki Station}} on the {{ill|San'yō Main Line|en|San'yō Main Line|qid=Q1193436|lt=Sanyo Main Line|1=San'yō Main Line}} * 2 minutes on foot from the Tamashima Chuocho bus stop == Related Articles == * {{ill|Haguro Shrine|ja|羽黒神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haguro Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Three Mountains of Dewa|en|Three Mountains of Dewa|qid=Q1155726|lt=Dewa Sanzan|1=Three Mountains of Dewa}} == External Links == * [https://hagurojinja.jimdofree.com/ Official Haguro Shrine Website] * [http://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/cgi-bin/jsearch.cgi?mode=detail&jcode=05003 Haguro Shrine - Okayama Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto navbox}} {{translated page|ja|羽黒神社 (倉敷市)|version=100913464|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:羽黒神社 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:%E7%BE%BD%E9%BB%92%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%80%89%E6%95%B7%E5%B8%82)]] {{wikidata link|Q11610436}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
